title: Documenting Regex Usage In API Filters
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I was having an issue with using the filters options (name key) provided by the /libpod/containers/json enpoint where it was incorrectly interpreting my filter and provided erronous results. After a bit of searching I came to realize that the name key value is actual interpreted as a regex expression. However this does not seem to be documented anywhere for that endpoint in the API [docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/ContainerListLibpod). With the more comprehensive documentation regarding filters residing [here](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-ps.1.html#filter-f). 

Additionally is there a resource detailing the specific regex operations supported (look-aheads, look-behinds, etc...) along with a way to specify a literal string that is not interpreted as regex?

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out, @p0da.  Can you give an example/reproducer?  I don't want to exclude the possibility that you may be hitting a bug.

I don't believe it is a bug but I certainly can provide an example:

Setup:
```bash
podman system service --time 100 unix:///tmp/podman.sock
podman run --name test alpine
podman run --name t alpine
```
While:
```bash
curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock 'http://d/v4.0.0/libpod/containers/json?all=true' --get --data-urlencode 'filters={"name": ["t"]}'
```
Returns all container names that begin with the letter t (both containers "t" and "test").

```bash
curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock 'http://d/v4.0.0/libpod/containers/json?all=true' --get --data-urlencode 'filters={"name": ["^t$"]}'
```
Returns only the container that is named "t".

The documentation of filter's usage of regex for some keys does not seem to exist at all in the API docs but does exist in the CLI docs.

Thanks, @p0da!  Podman behaves like this on the command line as well, just as Docker does. So it's really just something we need to document in the swagger docs.  The man pages state "accepts regex".  That's more helpful but does not match the level of detail you are requesting.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I certainly can but it may be better to overhaul sections of the API docs entirely as there seem to be some major issues (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13717) where things are just incorrectly documented along with various issues or omitted details that would be greatly helpful to include. Essentially uplifting the API docs to have detail parity with the man pages would be the goal in my opinion.
If you want to improve the API docs, that would be appreciated.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Hmm, I will see what I can do however it may take some time for me to get to it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
